name: -U
--upgrade
options.description: Upgrade all specified packages to the newest available version

name: --user
options.description: Install to the Python user install directory

name: --force-reinstall
options.description: Reinstall all packages even if already up-to-date

name: --no-deps
options.description: Do not install package dependencies

name: -r
--requirement
options.description: Install from the given requirements file

name: -e
--editable
options.description: Install a project in editable mode from a local path or VCS URL

name: --pre
options.description: Include pre-release and development versions

name: --no-cache-dir
options.description: Disable the cache

name: -t
--target
options.description: Install packages into this directory

name: --prefix
options.description: Installation prefix where lib, bin and other top-level folders are placed

name: --index-url
options.description: Base URL of the Python Package Index

name: --extra-index-url
options.description: Extra URLs of package indexes to use in addition to the index URL

name: --no-index
options.description: Ignore package index (only looking at --find-links URLs)

name: --find-links
options.description: Look for archives in this directory or on this HTML page

name: -c
--constraint
options.description: Constrain versions using the given constraints file

name: -y
--yes
options.description: Do not ask for confirmation of uninstall deletions

name: -r
--requirement
options.description: Uninstall all the packages listed in the given requirements file

subcommands.name: freeze
subcommands.description: Output installed packages in requirements format

name: -r
--requirement
options.description: Use the order in the given requirements file and its comments when generating output

name: -l
--local
options.description: If in a virtualenv that has global access, do not list globally-installed packages

name: --user
options.description: Only output packages installed in user-site

name: --all
options.description: Do not skip these packages in the output: setuptools, distribute, pip, wheel

name: --exclude-editable
options.description: Exclude editable package from output

name: --exclude
options.description: Exclude specified package from the output

name: -o
--outdated
options.description: List outdated packages

name: -u
--uptodate
options.description: List up-to-date packages

name: -l
--local
options.description: If in a virtualenv with global access, do not list globally-installed packages

name: --user
options.description: Only output packages installed in user-site

name: --pre
options.description: Include pre-release and development versions

name: --format
options.description: Select the output format (columns, freeze, json)

name: --not-required
options.description: List packages that are not dependencies of installed packages

name: --exclude-editable
options.description: Exclude editable package from output

name: --include-editable
options.description: Include editable package from output

name: --exclude
options.description: Exclude specified package from the output

name: -f
--files
options.description: Show the full list of installed files for each package

name: -d
--dest
options.description: Download packages into this directory

name: -r
--requirement
options.description: Download packages from the given requirements file

name: --platform
options.description: Only download wheels compatible with the platform

name: --python-version
options.description: Only download wheels compatible with the Python interpreter version

name: --no-deps
options.description: Do not download package dependencies

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Verify installed packages have compatible dependencies

name: -w
--wheel-dir
options.description: Build wheels into this directory, where the default is the current working directory

name: --no-deps
options.description: Do not build wheels for dependencies

name: -r
--requirement
options.description: Build wheels from the given requirements file

name: -a
--algorithm
options.description: The hash algorithm to use: sha256, sha384, sha512

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List the active configuration (or from the file specified in --file)

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Get the value associated with the name

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: Unset the value associated with the name

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Open the configuration file in an editor

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: List the configuration files and their contents

subcommands.name: cache
subcommands.description: Inspect and manage pip's wheel cache

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List filenames of packages stored in the cache

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove one or more package from the cache

subcommands.name: purge
subcommands.description: Remove all items from the cache

subcommands.name: inspect
subcommands.description: Inspect the python environment and print the results as JSON

name: --local
options.description: If in a virtualenv with global access, do not list globally-installed packages

name: --user
options.description: Only output packages installed in user-site

name: --path
options.description: Restrict to the specified installation path for listing packages

name: --platform
options.description: Only use wheels compatible with the platform

name: --python-version
options.description: Only use wheels compatible with the Python interpreter version
